title: Cynoglossum laevigatum
intro: Cynoglossum laevigatum, also known as smooth tongue flower, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum laevigatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is often used to treat coughs, colds, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Cynoglossum laevigatum is classified within the family Boraginaceae. This family comprises flowering plants commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. Cynoglossum laevigatum is further classified within the genus Cynoglossum, a group of flowering plants native to various parts of the world. The genus Cynoglossum is characterized by its hawksbeard-like appearance and the presence of five petals in its flowers. Cynoglossum laevigatum is often found growing in open areas and is native to New Zealand.
